source.caption: Épure E 61 (a) : rabattement du triangle ABC autour de la charnière horizontale ch^H, A étant sur la charnière.

notes: Ground line straightened to y=117.5: the plate's LT is drawn with ~1° of scan skew ((105,123)-(700,112)) while every recall line is pixel-vertical, so the drawing's own frame is axis-aligned and calibrating to the skewed LT would manufacture 10-16px recall mismatches that are not in the figure.
The hinge Ch^H is a horizontal line of the plane through A at height 0 (its V trace coincides with the LT, hence no aV/bV). C sits BELOW πH (C^V under the LT) — the plane crosses πH along the hinge.
B^V_R, C^V_R and O^V on the plate sit on the LT: rabattu points at height 0 project onto the ground line in V. Kept as a test invariant, not stored here.

The plate's own drawn C^H_R is imprecise: its swing from C^H drifts 17px ALONG the hinge, where a true rabattement swings exactly perpendicular. The reconstruction lands ~20px from it — that is the drawing's compass error, not a reading error.
